text.title: Agentic Token Pipeline and Gotchas
text.summary: Recommended agent pipeline for token alias generation, mode validation, and platform emission, plus critical agent-specific pitfalls.

rule.statement: Use agents for mechanical token operations (alias generation, mode-matrix expansion, contrast checks, format conversion); keep humans on naming and semantic decisions.
rule.rationale: Agents are reliable at deterministic transformations but poor at naming intent; auto-promoted primitive-to-semantic names are the one place where human meaning is irreplaceable.

list.item: Step 1: `token-aliaser` (haiku) — deterministic mapping of primitive to semantic from a rules file.
Step 2: `mode-validator` (haiku) — verifies every (alias × mode) cell has a value and meets contrast requirements.
Step 3: `multi-platform-emitter` — runs Style Dictionary to write CSS, iOS, Android, JSON.
Trigger: re-run on every Figma library version bump in CI.

rule.statement: Treat a token rename as a breaking API change; use codemods or alias deprecation periods, never silent removals.
rule.rationale: Consumers reference tokens as strings in code, config, and MDX; a silent removal breaks builds at runtime without compile-time warning.

section.title: Mode Coverage Check

rule.statement: Run the mode coverage check in CI on every PR that touches token source files; block on any missing (alias, mode) pair.
rule.rationale: A missing value detected in CI costs a fix; a missing value that ships costs a themed-product regression report, a hotfix, and user trust.

section.reference: Script that fails if any alias is missing a value in any declared mode.
Structured-output prompt for a mode-validator subagent including contrast checks.

antipattern.description: LLM invents token names from a closed enumeration without pinning to the manifest.
antipattern.reason: Pin the agent to a closed enumeration loaded from the project manifest; reject outputs with identifiers not present in the manifest.

antipattern.description: Color math regressions are silent: agent emits `oklch()` values that look fine but fail contrast in dark mode.
antipattern.reason: Always run the mode-validator post-generation; visual inspection is insufficient for contrast in wide-gamut color spaces.

antipattern.description: Figma Variables REST API uses `tempId` for newly-created variables; agent reuses a stale ID in a dependent operation.
antipattern.reason: Capture and reuse `tempId` values within the same API call batch; stale IDs create dangling references that are silent until the file is reloaded.

antipattern.description: Agent reorders Style Dictionary transforms while "cleaning up" the config.
antipattern.reason: Style Dictionary transforms run in declared order; reordering silently changes platform output. Lock the order in the agent's tooling spec.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-promotes primitives to semantic layer without human review.
antipattern.reason: Semantic naming is the one step that adds real human meaning; an auto-promoted name like `color.blue-500-alias` is worse than no semantic layer.
